No Poverty.reason: Econometrics provides tools for analyzing economic data, essential for measuring poverty (Target 1.2) and evaluating pro-poor policies (Target 1.b).
evidence: measuring empirical relationships between economic variables

Zero Hunger.reason: Analytical methods are applicable to measuring agricultural productivity (Target 2.3) and understanding food commodity markets (Target 2.c).

Good Health and Well-being.reason: Econometric analysis forms the methodological foundation for health economics, useful for evaluating health coverage and financing (Targets 3.8, 3.c).

Quality Education.reason: As a university-level course, it directly provides tertiary education (Target 4.3) and equips students with technical skills for employment (Target 4.4).

Gender Equality.reason: Econometric methods are used to analyze gender disparities, such as wage gaps, informing policies for equality (Targets 5.5, 5.c).

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No direct or indirect evidence found in the course syllabus.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: Analytical methods can be applied to energy economics, such as modeling energy efficiency (Target 7.3), though not an explicit focus.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course's core purpose is analyzing economic data and empirical relationships, fundamental for studying economic growth, productivity, and employment.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: Provides tools for analyzing economic data applicable to enhancing scientific research (Target 9.5) and measuring the economic impact of industrialization and infrastructure (Targets 9.1, 9.2).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course provides essential methods for analyzing economic data to measure income distribution (Target 10.1) and evaluate policy impacts on equality (Target 10.4).

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No direct or indirect evidence found in the course syllabus.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: Analytical skills can model resource efficiency and the decoupling of economic growth from environmental degradation, supporting Target 12.a.

Climate Action.reason: Econometric methods are foundational for climate economics, enabling analysis for integrating climate change measures into policies (Target 13.2).

Life Below Water.reason: No direct or indirect evidence found in the course syllabus.

Life on Land.reason: No direct or indirect evidence found in the course syllabus.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: Econometric methods can be applied to quantitatively study and develop effective, accountable, and transparent institutions (Target 16.6).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course directly builds statistical capacity, crucial for developing measurements of progress (Target 17.19) and enhancing data availability (Target 17.18).
